title: 10 precautions to take when using Rosmarinus officinalis (Rosemary) for medicinal purposes
intro: When using Rosmarinus officinalis, or rosemary, for medicinal purposes, it is important to take several precautions. First, rosemary should not be used by individuals with high blood pressure or seizure disorders, as it can lower blood pressure and potentially trigger seizures in susceptible people. Additionally, pregnant women should avoid using rosemary in medicinal amounts, as it may stimulate menstruation and potentially cause a miscarriage. It is also important to speak with a healthcare provider before using rosemary essential oil, as it is highly concentrated and can be toxic if used improperly. Finally, it is recommended to use rosemary under the guidance of a qualified healthcare practitioner, as proper dosages and methods of use are essential for ensuring safety and effectiveness.
